frames: The agent is in a low, crouched posture, with its front leg bent and back leg extended, making initial ground contact.
It begins to push forward, extending its front leg while the back leg remains bent, maintaining a low body position.
The agent's body is slightly elevated as both legs make contact, moving in a low, crawling-like gait.
It continues to propel itself forward, with the front leg lifting and extending, showing a somewhat jerky motion.
The agent's body drops very low, almost dragging on the ground, as its legs splay out in an attempt to move.
It briefly lifts its torso slightly, with both legs bent and pushing, but maintains a very low profile.
The body lowers again, with the front leg reaching forward and the back leg pushing, indicating an unstable gait.
The agent's body is nearly flat on the ground, with legs splayed, appearing to slide rather than walk effectively.
